Layout rule: Place the USB ESD array on FB-100 between the USB-C receptacle and JFB1 with the shortest practical path to the connector-side D+/D- pins; preferred ESD is USBLC6-2SC6 and alternate is TPD2EUSB30ADRTR
Evidence: FB-100-usb-service-closeout-precheck.csv; FB-100-component-sourcing-precheck.csv; ST USBLC6-2 datasheet; TI TPD2EUSB30 datasheet
Risk: Moving ESD near JFB1 would expose the connector-side trace stub to ESD stress and reduce field robustness
Blocked action: Do not create FB-100.kicad_pcb until the ESD footprint is bound and pin orientation is reviewed

Layout rule: Route USB_D_P and USB_D_N as a short coupled pair from USB-C to ESD to JFB1 pins 4 and 5; target 90 ohm differential where the final stackup permits; keep length mismatch at or below 1.0 mm; avoid stubs and unnecessary vias; keep continuous GND reference
Risk: The FFC path and prototype stackup may not be tightly impedance-controlled so keeping the pair short and referenced reduces signal-integrity risk
Blocked action: Do not route USB data through split reference planes or swap D+/D-

Layout rule: Route USB_VBUS through R13 3.9 kOhm to USB_VBUS_DETECT_RAW with R14 15 kOhm and C1 100 nF returned directly to GND near JFB1/U14; do not pour VBUS and do not connect VBUS to FB_3V3_OR_IO PB_5V_OUT LB_3V3_IO or PB-100 rails
Risk: Removing R14 leaves a floating state while any low-impedance VBUS path can back-power LB-100 or PB-100
Blocked action: Do not remove the raw pulldown or connect USB VBUS to any system power rail

Layout rule: Keep USB_CC1 and USB_CC2 as device-role Rd behavior only; no Rp source pull-up no dual-role source path no power advertisement and no source current path from FB-100
Risk: Wrong CC policy can make the service port advertise unsafe source behavior or hide cable attach state
Blocked action: Do not add Rp source-power or dual-role behavior without ADR

Layout rule: Route USB shell and ESD return to a local ESD/chassis return strategy and FB ground reference without using USB shield as signal return or high-current return; keep final RC/0R/DNP shell network as schematic-footprint review item
Risk: An undefined shield return can inject ESD into logic ground or create noise and service-cable reliability issues
Blocked action: Do not use USB shield as a high-current return or leave ESD return undefined

Layout rule: Keep USB_D_P and USB_D_N adjacent through the FFC transition and near JFB1 pins 4 and 5 with nearby ground reference pins 1 and 3; preserve pinout and do not remap USB pins for routing convenience
Risk: Changing JFB1 pin order would break LB-100 compatibility and turn the cable into a custom variant
Blocked action: Do not change JFB1 pinout without ADR-backed interface review

Layout rule: Before any board import audit USB_VBUS_DETECT_RAW as R13-current-limited R14-defined-low detect-only and verify that U14 Ioff prevents a DC path to FB_3V3_OR_IO PB_5V_OUT LB_3V3_IO PB-100 or any output rail
Risk: Back-power or a floating presence indication can appear unless the exact topology is audited
Blocked action: Do not release layout review until the no-back-power and defined-low net audit is recorded

Layout rule: This USB/no-back-power layout model is documentation-only and creates no KiCad PCB layout Gerbers drill files pick-place files BOM/CPL order package manufacturing ZIP fabrication package or PCBA order
Risk: Documentation closure without footprint binding still cannot prove manufacturable USB placement
Blocked action: Do not generate JLCPCB/PCBWay outputs from this closeout
